channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia prioritises affordable medicine despite looming US tariffs on pharmaceuticals
item.description: Starting July 31, the United States will impose tariffs of up to 100% on some pharmaceutical imports, including Australian-made products. However, Australia’s Health Minister Mark Butler has assured t...

item.title: Jammu-Srinagar NH-44 Closed Due to Landslides and Heavy Rain
item.description: The Jammu-Srinagar National Highway (NH-44), the sole all-weather road connecting Kashmir to the rest of India, continued to remain closed for the third straight day due to persistent heavy rainfall a...

item.title: Why Nigeria’s Fintech Needs Local Languages to Achieve True Inclusion
item.description: This article talk about one important challenge inside Nigeria’s growing fintech space — language accessibility. Even though financial technology don grow strong with speed and reach, almost all the d...

item.title: Chemical fallout from strikes on Tehran and Arak raises civilian and environmental fears
item.description: The article argues that recent US-Israeli strikes on Iran have caused severe chemical and environmental harm, especially in Tehran and Arak. It says attacks on fuel depots, oil reservoirs, an aluminiu...

item.title: Longevity Clinics Under Fire for Unproven Treatments and Health Risks
item.description: A recent article in The New York Times highlights the burgeoning field of longevity clinics and the growing concerns surrounding their practices. While the promise of extending lifespan and combating ...

item.title: Political Leaders Protest Against INEC’s Alleged Partisanship in Abuja
item.description: On April 8, 2026, prominent Nigerian leaders including Atiku Abubakar, Peter Obi, Rotimi Amaechi, and other political figures led a protest in Abuja, demanding reforms within the Independent National ...

item.title: Vodacom upgrades M-Pesa with $28 million to improve reliability and security
item.description: Vodacom Tanzania has completed a major upgrade of its M-Pesa mobile money system following an investment of $28 million, roughly Sh72 billion. The upgrade moves M-Pesa from the old G2 system to a next...

item.title: Ramaphosa issues diplomatic guidelines, warns against public criticism of South Africa
item.description: In a formal ceremony at the Sefako Makgatho Presidential Guesthouse in Pretoria, President Cyril Ramaphosa presented credentials to 20 new ambassadors, including US Ambassador Leo Brent Bozell. During...

item.title: Jan Vishwas 2.0 Introduces Trust-Oriented Compliance Framework for Businesses
item.description: The Indian Parliament has recently passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, marking a significant shift in the regulatory landscape for businesses. The reform prioritises trust-bas...

item.title: Quebec Legislature Pauses as Coalition Avenir Québec Chooses New Premier
item.description: The Quebec government has temporarily prorogued the National Assembly as the Coalition Avenir Québec (CAQ) prepares to select a new party leader, who will also assume the role of premier. This follows...

item.title: Locking Up Kids Fails to Improve Safety or Reduce Reoffending
item.description: Locking up children does not improve community safety, yet punitive youth justice policies persist across Australia. Evidence shows that incarceration, especially for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Isla...

item.title: Accused Arrested in 2014 Villivakkam Murder Case After Failing to Appear for Trial
item.description: Chennai city police have successfully apprehended an accused involved in a 2014 murder case registered at the Villivakkam police station. The accused, Satheesh alias Yugalinkam, had been previously ar...

item.title: Mark Jenkin premieres time-travel film Rose of Nevada in Newlyn
item.description: Cornish filmmaker Mark Jenkin premiered his latest ghostly time-travel film, Rose of Nevada, in his home town of Newlyn, Cornwall. The screening at the Newlyn Filmhouse, an arthouse cinema housed in a...

item.title: Michael Moore Criticizes Democratic Leadership Over U.S.-Iran Tensions
item.description: Oscar-winning filmmaker Michael Moore strongly criticized Democratic leaders in a recent Substack post, calling them 'cowards' and accusing them of supporting President Donald Trump's approach toward ...

item.title: PMDC allows temporary 3% relaxation in MDCAT passing marks to fill unoccupied medical and dental seats
item.description: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) has allowed universities to reduce the Medical and Dental College Admission Test (MDCAT) passing percentage by up to three per cent for the 2025–26 acade...

item.title: Kenya Bankers Recommend CBK Maintain Policy Rate at 8.75% to Mitigate External Risks
item.description: The Kenya Bankers Association (KBA) has called on the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) to maintain the benchmark policy rate at 8.75 percent, citing concerns over rising global risks that could push inflat...

item.title: Eli Lilly's MD in ANZ Pushes for PBS Listing of Weight-Loss Drug
item.description: Manny Simons, now managing director of Eli Lilly in Australia and New Zealand, has a remarkable background blending science, business, and the arts. Holding a neuroscience and music degree from Harvar...

item.title: Alameda Healthcare Expands Its Operations to Kenya with New Specialized Clinic
item.description: Alameda Healthcare, an Egyptian healthcare provider, has launched a new clinic in Nairobi, marking its first step into the Kenyan market. The new facility, situated at Landmark Plaza, will offer speci...

item.title: How an Iron Ring Shaped a Career Beyond Engineering
item.description: In this personal reflection, Ben Balevi recounts his experience receiving the Iron Ring during his engineering graduation at McGill University in 1982. The ceremony, rooted in the tragic collapse of t...

item.title: Ceasefire in Iran brings temporary relief to global financial markets, but risks remain
item.description: Financial markets have experienced significant relief following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire in the ongoing Iran conflict, with oil prices dropping and stock markets seeing a rally. Howeve...
